summary.brief: NEVINS, M.D. v. MARTYN C/W 85247/85541/85596. Medical malpractice case involving surgery orthopaedic.

evidence_index.text: In 2009, Dr. Russell Nevins performed a revision right total shoulder arthroplasty on Marilyn Martyn. During this shoulder replacement surgery, Martyn suffered a fracture to her distal humerus.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Nevins attempted to cement the fracture, but the cement extravasated through the fracture in the distal humerus and caused a burning injury to Martyn's radial nerve, resulting in permanent radial nerve palsy.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The district court did not abuse its discretion when it gave a res ipsa loquitur jury instruction notwithstanding the expert testimony.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: We affirm the underlying negligence judgment against appellants.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: The jury found in favor of Martyn. Although the jury determined that damages were warranted in the amounts of $1,576,800 for past pain and suffering and $1,576,800 for future pain and suffering, the district court reduced the total amount for pain and suffering to $350,000 pursuant to NRS 41A.035.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: We conclude that professional entities are subject to the cap when they are vicariously liable for a listed individual medical practitioner's negligence.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's analysis

evidence_index.text: We affirm the district court's attorney fees award as modified, because relief is not warranted regarding the district court's Beattie and Brunzell analyses, but the district court abused its discretion in failing to apply NRS 7.095 to appellant professional entities.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: Because NRS 7.095 applies to professional entities, the attorney fees award against appellant professional entities must be reduced.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's analysis

evidence_index.text: Accordingly, we conclude that both NRCP 68 and NRS 18.005 apply here.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: The district court abused its discretion in summarily applying the Frazier factors pursuant to NRS 18.020, and therefore further proceedings as to the expert witness fees are warranted.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's analysis

initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Patient underwent a revision right total shoulder arthroplasty.

requestables.description: Imaging to assess the status of the shoulder post-arthroplasty

reveal.clinical_significance: Indicates a complication from the surgery
reveal.decision_impact: Should prompt consideration of surgical repair or alternative management

timeline_phases.name: Initial Presentation
timeline_phases.description: Patient presents for revision shoulder arthroplasty

timeline_phases.name: Surgical Procedure
timeline_phases.description: Revision right total shoulder arthroplasty performed

decision_points.clinical_context: During revision right total shoulder arthroplasty
decision_points.prompt: How should the fracture of the distal humerus be managed?

options.clinical_reasoning: Standard practice to ensure stability and prevent complications
Cement extravasation risk leading to nerve injury

actual_action_defendant.description: Defendant attempted to cement the fracture

expected_action_court.description: Fracture should have been secured with appropriate fixation
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Expert evidence established fixation as standard

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Cement extravasation caused radial nerve injury
explanation.what_should_have_happened: Fracture should have been fixed with hardware
explanation.legal_standard_applied: Standard of care as established by expert testimony

patient_outcome.description: Patient suffered permanent radial nerve palsy

key_findings: Breach of duty in failing to properly manage fracture
Causation established - cement extravasation caused nerve injury

malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Proper fixation would have prevented nerve injury

factual_timeline.event: Dr. Nevins performed revision right total shoulder arthroplasty
